div.h1: DIRTY PAGE TRACKING AND FLUSH DATA FLOW
div.p: Write-back caching architecture: modifications accumulate in volatile memory. Persistence is deferred until explicit flush or cache pressure.

ol.li: VM Layer operates strictly on pointers to frame->data.
Dirty Bit is the only indicator that memory and disk have diverged.
Writes are asynchronous relative to the VM execution, unless eviction occurs.

div.p: Data Integrity: This order ensures that if a crash occurs during DB write, the Rollback Journal or WAL contains the recovery data.

g.title: Entry point for data modification. Triggers B-Tree balance logic.
Byte-level mutation happens here after marking the page dirty in the pager.
Check if the process holds the appropriate filesystem lock before modification.
Write-Ahead-Log entry must be generated before the in-memory page is ever modified.
The dirty bit is set in the page header structure (64B metadata).
Memory Address: 0x7F001000 Data: Consistent with Disk
sizeof(Frame) = 4096 (data) + 64 (meta) bytes
Memory Address: 0x7F003080 Data: Pending persistence
Memory Address: 0x7F0040C0 Data: Modified in-situ
Minimizes disk head movement by scheduling writes in order of file offset.
System call: Direct I/O to specific file offset.
Flushes OS write cache to physical media controller.
Buffer pool is full; must evict to make room for new page.
WAL must be durable before the 'data' page reaches the main DB file.
Synchronous flush during query execution (High latency path).
Offset: 0x0000
Offset: 0x1000
Offset: 0x2000
Offset: 5 * 4096 = 20,480 bytes
